title: Well Animal Checkups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Well Animal Checkups 10 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Homelessness Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF FOR THE LOVE OF PAWS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides low cost veterinary services including checkups, emergency services, and spay/neuter services. Services includes digital x-rays (including dental), ultrasound, lab equipment (bloodwork/urinalysis), and laser therapy. 725 SE Mosher Avenue Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Low income families and families of veterans and service members Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-5pm, Friday 9am-noon Email (541) 677-6070 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FRIENDS OF THE ANIMALS FOTAS MONTHLY LOW-COST VACCINATION AND MICROCHIP CLINIC MONTHLY VACCINATION AND MICROCHIP CLINICS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a low-cost vaccination and microchip clinic for community members dogs and cats. The vaccines available are rabies, cat distemper/PanLeuk/upper respiratory, dog distemper/Parvo/upper respiratory, and Bordatella. Microchipping services offered. All services are by appointment only and appointments must be made for each individual animal. 300 W Valley View Road Talent, OR 97540 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 3rd Saturday of the month Email (541) 708-0090 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COAST HUMANE SOCIETY OCHS VET CLINIC CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides veterinary services including spay and neuter procedures, wellness exams, microchipping and vaccinations, bloodwork, imaging, and ultrasound. Low-cost and no-cost care vouchers may be available. 1739 22nd Street Florence, OR 97439 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours, by appointment only Email (541) 590-3333 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HUMANE SOCIETY COMMUNITY VETERINARY HOSPITAL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides veterinary services including preventative care, dentistry, spay and neuter services, and urgent care. Also helps with end-of-life care and pet memorial services. 7865 NE 14th Place Portland, OR 97211 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 7 days per week 7:30am-5:30pm by appointment only Email (503) 285-7722 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PET HELP FINDER PET RESOURCES LOCATOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Locator for veterinary services, pet food pantry, supplies, pet services such as boarding, co-housing shelters, pet care for seniors, transportation support, and behavioral help. Locator also lists grant programs, free and discounted services, and pop-up events such as vaccine clinics. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email Visit Website More Details PORTLAND ANIMAL WELFARE TEAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides veterinary health care clinics by appointment and access to a pet supply bank. Households can access the pet supply bank monthly without appointments or pre-qualification. Veterinary care is offered by appointments or walk-in pre-scheduled drop-in clinic dates. Dates of drop-in clinics are available on website and phone voicemail. Information can also be provided in person during regular hours. Does not offer emergency, urgent, or walk-in veterinary care outside of pre-scheduled drop-in clinic dates. At veterinary clinics, maximum of two pets per household will be seen. Services include: -Basic health exams -Vaccines -Flea and de-worming treatments -Pet supplies -Discounted animal licensing Accepts donations of new and gently used pet supplies and some medications. Check website for specific donation requirements. Cannot accept: Any food past one year of expiration, CBD treats or supplements, retractable leashes, shock collars, electric collars, or prong collars. Volunteer opportunities are available. 1718 NE 82nd Avenue Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Pets of people who are experiencing homelessness or extreme poverty. Monthly income must be below the federal poverty level. Pets must be spayed/neutered or client must be willing to spay/neuter their pet. County Licensing: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington County residents Volunteers: Age 16 and older Hours: Tuesday-Thursday 10am-3pm Veterinary care: By appointment only Vaccine and Wellness Clinics: Varies Email (503) 206-6033 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PRO-BONE-O VETERINARY CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Volunteer-run clinics provide medical exams, vaccinations, flea treatments, and vouchers for spay/neuter services. Services are provided on a first-come, first-serve basis, and individuals are limited to two pets per person. Assistance is dependent upon available abilities and not guaranteed. 1509 W 1st Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Individuals who are homeless Hours: 2nd and 4th Sundays of the month, 9am-1pm. Gates open at 8:45am and the last patients will be admitted at noon. Email (541) 799-0441 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFEHAVEN HUMANE SOCIETY PET WELLNESS CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a preventative care wellness clinic that includes low-cost preventative care services such as vaccines, flea control, dewormer, and microchips for both dogs and cats. 32220 Old Highway 34 Tangent, OR 973899770 Eligibility: Unrestricted. All pets must be safely handleable by medical team. Any pets that are a risk to staff safely will not be eligible to receive services. Not able to take in feral cats for any services. Hours: 1st Friday of the month 10am-2pm Email (541) 928-2789 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH COAST HUMANE SOCIETY LOW COST WELLNESS AND VACCINE CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a pet wellness clinic offering exams, vaccinations, microchips, ear and gland cleaning, nail trimming, and flea medication. 828 Railroad Street Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Visit website for current schedule Email (541) 412-0325 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VETERANS STAND DOWNS AND VETERANS EVENTS STAND DOWN EVENTS VETERANS STAND DOWN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an annual health and wellness resource event. Services may include health screening, medical and dental care, vision, housing resources, counseling and substance abuse referrals, haircuts, pet care, job fairs, help accessing VA services and benefits, DMV and legal assistance. Meals, clothing, and hygiene supplies may also be offered. Eligibility: Veterans and military personnel; focus is generally on homeless and at-risk veterans Hours: Varies by event Visit Website More Details FOR THE LOVE OF PAWS FOR THE LOVE OF PAWS FRIENDS OF THE ANIMALS MONTHLY VACCINATION AND MICROCHIP CLINICS OREGON COAST HUMANE SOCIETY CLINIC OREGON HUMANE SOCIETY COMMUNITY VETERINARY HOSPITAL PORTLAND ANIMAL WELFARE TEAM PORTLAND ANIMAL WELFARE TEAM PRO-BONE-O VETERINARY CARE SAFEHAVEN HUMANE SOCIETY PET WELLNESS CLINIC SOUTH COAST HUMANE SOCIETY LOW COST WELLNESS AND VACCINE CLINIC
